## The Math

Given the final hidden state $h_t = X_{\text{final}}[T-1] \in \mathbb{R}^d$:

*Step 1 — Final Layer Norm:*

$$
\tilde{h}_t = \operatorname{LayerNorm}(h_t)    \in \mathbb{R}^d
$$

*Step 2 — Vocabulary Projection:*

$$
\text{logits} = \tilde{h}_t W_u    \in \mathbb{R}^{|V|}
$$

With weight tying: $W_u = E^{\top}$, so:

$$
\text{logits}[i] = \tilde{h}_t \cdot E_i
$$

The logit for token `i` is the dot product of the model's "prediction vector" with token `i`'s embedding.
Tokens whose embeddings align with the prediction vector get high logits.

*Step 3 — Softmax → Probabilities:*

$$
P(\text{next} = i \mid \text{context}) = \operatorname{softmax}(\text{logits})[i] = \frac{\exp(\text{logits}[i])}{\sum_j \exp(\text{logits}[j])}
$$

::: {.callout-note}
*Math Minute — Temperature*

We can control the "sharpness" of the distribution with a *temperature parameter* $T_{\text{temp}}$:

$$
P(i) = \operatorname{softmax}(\text{logits} / T_{\text{temp}})[i]
$$

- $T_{\text{temp}} \to 0$: argmax — always pick the highest-probability token (greedy)
- $T_{\text{temp}} = 1$: standard softmax
- $T_{\text{temp}} > 1$: flattens the distribution — more randomness/creativity

Temperature is not a model parameter — it's a sampling hyperparameter set at inference time.
:::
